page.content: In the competitive landscape of the Asia-Pacific retail sector, Costco Wholesale has strategically enhanced its customer loyalty program, evidenced by key performance metrics that underscore its effectiveness in driving member engagement and sales growth.

lines.text: In the competitive landscape of the Asia-Pacific retail sector, Costco Wholesale has strategically enhanced its customer loyalty
program, evidenced by key performance metrics that underscore its effectiveness in driving member engagement and sales

page.content: In Q1 2025, the program recorded a notable increase in New Sign-ups, reaching 150,000, which reflects ongoing interest in membership benefits and incentives. Moreover, the loyalty program has successfully maintained a Churn Rate of 5.0%, indicating a strong retention strategy that encourages repeat patronage. The financial metrics associated with the program reveal a robust Average Transaction value of ¥3,600, suggesting that engaged members tend to spend more per visit. The Repeat Purchase Rate further exemplifies this trend, standing at an impressive 77.5%, which highlights the effectiveness of loyalty initiatives in fostering consistent consumer behavior. However, the investment in the loyalty program has incurred Program Costs amounting to ¥480M, necessitating ongoing analysis to ensure sustainable profitability.

page.content: As Costco continues to innovate its offerings, these metrics will be crucial in evaluating the impact of the loyalty program on overall sales growth and customer satisfaction in the region.
